key_insight: The paper maps the 2D periodic incompressible Stokes equations into a quantum spectral basis where the Laplacian is diagonal and incompressibility is enforced by a mode-by-mode Helmholtz projection.
engineering_relevance: It offers a polylog-grid quantum solver concept for flow mechanics that could accelerate periodic Stokes solves and multiscale RVE-style homogenization workflows.
method: Use the Quantum Fourier Transform to move from physical space to Fourier modes.
Apply a longitudinal/transverse rotation per mode to enforce divergence-free velocity.
Implement inverse-Laplacian and pressure-velocity splitting with polynomially encoded amplitude blocks.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports numerical verification on a steady vortex, a periodic force-dipole benchmark, and an RVE-inspired Kolmogorov-like fluctuation case, including recovery of a homogenized kinetic-energy observable.
watch_item: Practical performance still depends on efficient state preparation, observable estimation, and approximation degree/tile count, which may dominate real hardware cost.
paper_title: A Quantum Spectral Solver for Periodic Incompressible Stokes Flow
